title: Victor Wembanyama Out For Game 3 As Concussion Recovery Continues
summary: San Antonio Spurs center Victor Wembanyama did not play Friday in Game 3 against the Portland Trail Blazers while continuing to work through the NBA's concussion protocol. Wembanyama exited Game 2 in the second quarter and did not return after he hit his head on the floor driving to the besket. Portland won that game 106-103 in San Antonio. "Victor is not playing tonight. Obviously, there's a lot that goes into that, but he's doing well and progressing," Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said. Luke Kornet started at center in Wembanyama's place. The veteran big man appeared in 68 regular-season games this year with 25 starts, averaging 6.5 points and 6.1 rebounds. Wembanyama traveled with the team to Portland on Thursday and was working on his shot at Friday morning's shootaround at the Moda Center. Players must clear a series of neurological benchmarks, measured against preseason baseline evaluations, before receiving clearance to return under league protocol. San Antonio went 12-6 in regular-season games without Wembanyama, providing some reason for optimism during his recovery. Johnson said further details about Wembanyama's condition would remain internal.

title: NBA Local Viewership Holds Steady Year-Over-Year After 12 Percent Drop In 2024-25
summary: NBA local viewership remained essentially flat compared to the prior season in 2025-26, but league officials and media analysts view that stability as a meaningful achievement amid accelerating cord-cutting trends that cost the NBA 12 percent of its in-market audience just one year earlier. The Denver Nuggets, Detroit Pistons, Philadelphia 76ers, Phoenix Suns and New York Knicks recorded the largest year-over-year local audience gains across the league, according to Nielsen Station Index data. Detroit led all NBA franchises in streaming growth. The Pistons, led by Cade Cunningham's second consecutive All-Star selection and an Eastern Conference-best 60-22 record, drew a surge of digital viewers throughout the season. Streaming now accounts for 15% of total NBA local deliveries, up sharply from just 5% in 2023-24. The league recorded a 43% increase in unique streamers per game, with average minutes streamed climbing 37% versus the prior season. The gains offset continued erosion of traditional pay-TV subscribers. Bankruptcy court projections filed by Diamond Sports, predecessor to the now-dissolving Main Street Sports Group, forecast a 26% decline in linear TV subscribers by 2027 and a 47% drop in local distribution revenues during the same period. "We're going to be seeing a great deal of movement and experimentation as Main Street winds down their business," said Craig Sloan, CEO of Playfly Sports, during a recent Zoom interview. "There's a lot of upheaval in the space, but when you look back at the last 10 years of local audience deliveries, live sports haven't lost a step."

title: Knicks Open Door To Removing Mikal Bridges From Starting Lineup For Game 4
summary: New York Knicks coach Mike Brown said Friday that a starting lineup change remains possible for Saturday's Game 4 against the Atlanta Hawks, declining to commit to any specific configuration one day after they took a 2-1 series lead. "At this point in the year, anything is on the table, what we do offensively, what we do defensively, what our rotations are, who starts, what we come out with," Brown said. "Everything is on the table." Mikal Bridges is the focal point of the discussion and has connected on just 8 of 22 shots through three games, was held scoreless in Game 3, committed four turnovers and was removed from the rotation for all but two minutes of the second half. Miles McBride is the leading candidate to replace Bridges in the starting five. The unit of Jalen Brunson, OG Anunoby, Josh Hart, Karl-Anthony Towns and McBride posted a plus-24 net rating across 14 minutes in Game 3, a game New York lost by a single point. Brunson, Bridges, Anunoby, Hart and Towns recorded a 2.3 net rating across 541 regular-season minutes together, the second-most-used lineup in the NBA this past season. In the playoffs, that same group carries a minus-7.4 net rating. "I just got to be better so I can be out there," Bridges said Thursday. Any adjustment would mark the first lineup alteration since the season's opening weeks, when Josh Hart replaced Mitchell Robinson in the starting unit at Hart's own suggestion to then-coach Tom Thibodeau. New York faces elimination pressure if it drops Game 4, trailing the series 2-1.

title: Michigan Lands 7-Foot-2 Transfer Moustapha Thiam From Cincinnati
summary: Cincinnati transfer center Moustapha Thiam, rated the No. 11 overall player in ESPN's transfer portal rankings, has committed to Michigan, he told ESPN's Shams Charania on Friday. The 7-foot-2 big man visited coach Dusty May and the reigning national champions earlier this week. Thiam also made official visits to St. John's, Arkansas and Kansas before selecting Michigan. He was considered a top-five available player remaining in the portal at the time of his commitment. The Senegal native averaged 12.8 points, 7.1 rebounds and 1.6 blocks across his sophomore season with the Bearcats. He finished the year on a dominant stretch, posting 18.0 points, 10.4 rebounds and 1.9 blocks over his final eight games following a February foot injury. Thiam's late-season production included 28 points and eight rebounds against Kansas, 24 points and 15 rebounds against Oklahoma State, and 21 points and 10 rebounds against Texas Tech. Before Cincinnati, the 2024 top-30 recruit spent his freshman year at UCF, averaging 10.4 points, 6.4 rebounds and 2.6 blocks in 34 games. Thiam gives May his third portal big man this spring, joining J.P. Estrella from Tennessee and Jalen Reed from LSU. The trio will anchor Michigan's frontcourt following the anticipated departures of projected first-round picks Aday Mara and Morez Johnson Jr. Michigan has also added five-star guard Brandon McCoy Jr. and remains in pursuit of high-scoring Wake Forest transfer Juke Harris, who is simultaneously navigating the NBA draft process.

title: Kevin Durant Eyes NBA Europe Ownership Through PSG Investment
summary: Houston Rockets forward Kevin Durant holds a minority stake in Paris Saint-Germain, whose ownership group is actively pursuing a franchise in NBA Europe, the projected 2027-28 international league. The investment could give the 16-time All-Star a foothold in basketball ownership while still under contract as a player. Durant purchased his PSG stake on June 21, following a relationship with club chairman Nasser Al-Khelaifi that formed during the 2024 Paris Olympics. "This was in the cards. This was in play, PSG Basketball," Durant said. "They want more of an imprint in the United States. They want more of their brand recognized around the world, not just in Europe." NBA Europe is projected to feature 12 permanent franchises and four annually qualified clubs, with entry fees estimated between $500 million and $1 billion, per Sports Business Journal. NBA deputy commissioner Mark Tatum confirmed in April that the league and FIBA have begun reviewing bids from prospective ownership groups. Current collective bargaining rules bar active NBA players from investing in NBA teams, though no such restriction applies to NBA Europe franchises. One prominent agent told Andscape that active players will likely be permitted minority, but not controlling, stakes in NBA Europe clubs. Adam Silver acknowledged the complexity Durant's dual role presents, noting the league would need to establish a framework with the players' association if a player held equity in an NBA Europe ownership group. Durant is not alone in his positioning as Luka Doncic is part of an investor group seeking a Rome-based NBA Europe franchise. LeBron James holds an ownership stake in Liverpool FC, which has separately expressed interest in NBA Europe. Giannis Antetokounmpo has also indicated openness to ownership in both NBA Europe and the Basketball Africa League. "Anything that can connect the NBA through the rest of the world, a structured league, hell yeah I'm down for it," Durant said.

title: First-Round Of NBA Playoffs Tighter Than Expected As Betting Markets Reflect Probability Swings
summary: Through the early stages of the NBA playoffs, betting markets have recorded sharp probability swings across multiple first-round matchups, with all but two lower seeds outperforming expectations heading into Games 3 and 4. This is largely due to five of the series being tied at 1-1 after the first two games. The Los Angeles Lakers posted the largest single-series swing, jumping 40.9 percentage points after taking a 2-0 lead over the Houston Rockets after entering as heavy underdogs at +400 as they navigate without Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves. The Atlanta Hawks shifted from a 26.7% opening probability to 53.1%, now holding a 2-1 series lead over the New York Knicks. The Denver Nuggets have also experienced a sharp decline against a Minnesota Timberwolves team they tried to avoid in the first round. The Nuggets' playoff survival odds have dropped 27.8 percentage points following their Game 3 defeat, leaving them in a near coin-flip scenario against Minnesota at even odds. The Oklahoma City Thunder remain the dominant favorite, with their implied win probability rising from 96.8% to 99.8% after two games against the Phoenix Suns. In the East, the Cleveland Cavaliers stands as the only top seed to strengthen its position, with markets now pricing the Cavaliers at a 92.3% probability to advance past the Toronto Raptors despite losing Game 3. title: Mikal Bridges Benched For Most Of Second Half In Knicks' Game 3 Loss At Hawks
summary: Mikal Bridges logged a career playoff low of just 21 minutes as the New York Knicks dropped Game 3 to the Atlanta Hawks 109-108 Thursday, falling behind 2-1 in the first-round series. Mike Brown pulled Bridges early in the third quarter, replacing him with Miles McBride. Bridges returned only for Atlanta's final defensive possession, which ended in a CJ McCollum pull-up jumper giving the Hawks a one-point edge with 12.5 seconds remaining. Bridges finished scoreless and posted a minus-26 rating with four turnovers. His struggles follow a similarly poor second half in Game 2, when he went scoreless with a minus-11 rating as Atlanta mounted a fourth-quarter rally to seize home-court advantage. "I'm not concerned about him," Brown said. "I just went with what I felt the game called for that time. Mikal's a pro. He's played hundreds of basketball games, so he should be fine." OG Anunoby led New York with 29 points and nine rebounds. McBride contributed five three-pointers, while Jalen Brunson added 26 points despite missing all five of his three-point attempts. Brunson was targeted defensively by Atlanta for the second straight game. New York shot just 29% from three-point range compared to Atlanta's 39%. Jonathan Kuminga delivered 21 points off the bench for the Hawks, repeatedly catching the Knicks off-balance with his quickness and energy. "I've got to take this on the chin, handle it how I'm supposed to and be ready for the next one," Bridges said. "You know it's going to suck. It is what it is. I've just got to be better to help my team out there."

title: Wolves Win Game 3 Over Nuggets, Hold Nikola Jokic To Worst Shooting Night Of Playoff Career
summary: Nikola Jokic endured the worst shooting night of his playoff career Thursday, connecting on just 7 of 26 field goal attempts as the Minnesota Timberwolves defeated the Denver Nuggets 113-96 to claim a 2-1 first-round series lead. The loss marked the first time since January that Denver failed to surpass 100 points. The Nuggets, who posted the second-best offensive rating in NBA history at 121.2 this regular season, were held scoreless at an alarming rate from the opening tip. Denver managed only 3-of-21 shooting in the first quarter, the team's lowest-scoring quarter all season. Minnesota established a 27-point advantage and held Denver under a double-digit deficit for the final three periods. Rudy Gobert anchored Minnesota's defensive scheme, matching Jokic's minutes throughout the night. Jokic went 7-of-21 against Gobert in Game 3 and is now 8-of-29 against him across the two Nuggets losses. Jokic is also 5-of-24 on three-point attempts in the series. Jamal Murray shot 5-of-17 from the field with five missed three-pointers. Murray is 15-of-42 shooting across both Denver defeats. "He had a tough night," said Nuggets coach David Adelman. "It happens to players. This guy's played a million playoff games. There's nights that are poor. He'll bounce back. Everyone needs a day to understand we didn't play well offensively." Denver was also without starting forward Aaron Gordon, ruled out two hours before tipoff with a calf injury. Wing Peyton Watson remains sidelined since April 1 with a hamstring strain. Both players will receive treatment Friday ahead of Game 4. "I've never seen him shoot what he shot today," Murray said. "That's an outlier type of game." Despite losses in the past two games and mounting injury concerns, the Nuggets are -2.5 point favorites in Saturday's Game 4 . Minnesota are now slightly favored to win the series with an implied probability of 55 percent.

summary: Phoenix Suns guard Devin Booker has been fined $35,000 for public criticism of the officiating. Booker made his comments to the press following the Suns' 120-107 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 2 of their first-round playoff series on April 22 at Paycom Center. "In my 11 years, I haven't called a ref out by name, but James [Williams] was terrible tonight through and through," Booker said on Wednesday . "It's bad for the sport, bad for the integrity of the sport. People are going to start viewing this as a WWE if they're not held responsible." The NBA also determined that Booker's technical foul at 2:05 in the third quarter was improperly assessed and it has therefore been rescinded.

summary: Oklahoma City Thunder forward Jalen Williams will miss at least the next three games of the team's first-round series against the Phoenix Suns after sustaining a Grade 1 left hamstring strain, the team announced Thursday. Williams will be evaluated on a weekly basis. The injury occurred with 6:26 remaining in the third quarter of Wednesday's 120-107 Game 2 victory. Williams grabbed his left leg after missing a fast-break layup attempt, subsequently took a deliberate foul to exit the game and went directly to the locker room. He did not address reporters before departing Paycom Center. Williams is ruled out for Saturday's Game 3, Monday's Game 4 and a potential Wednesday Game 5. The timing is a significant blow despite Oklahoma City's commanding series position. Williams had been outstanding to open the postseason after a regular season limited to 33 games due to injury. He posted 22 points, seven rebounds and six assists in Game 1 and followed with 19 points on 7-of-11 shooting in 23 minutes in Game 2. "He was playing an outstanding game," coach Mark Daigneault said. "He was on the gas. He had great force. He was hitting shots." Williams, a third-team All-NBA selection last season, played through a torn scapholunate ligament in his right wrist throughout last year's championship run before undergoing surgery. He also missed extended stretches this season with a right hamstring strain. Oklahoma City went 39-10 without Williams in the lineup this regular season.

title: Derrick White Wins 2025-26 NBA Sportsmanship Award
summary: Boston Celtics guard Derrick White has been selected as the 2025-26 NBA Sportsmanship Award recipient . White claims the Joe Dumars Trophy for the first time in his nine-year career. The honor marks consecutive seasons in which a Celtic has earned the award as Jrue Holiday received the trophy last season. While Holiday is a two-time winner, Mike Conley has won the award four times. Current NBA players cast votes to determine the winner from six divisional finalists. A panel of league executives selected those finalists from 30 team nominees, one per club. The Joe Dumars Trophy has been presented annually since the 1995-96 season. It honors the player who best exemplifies sportsmanship on the court and is named for Naismith Basketball Hall of Famer Joe Dumars, who won the inaugural award and spent his entire 14-year career with the Detroit Pistons. White brings a decorated resume to the recognition. A two-time NBA All-Defensive Team selection, he won an NBA championship with Boston in 2023-24 and captured a gold medal with the USA Men's National Team at the 2024 Paris Olympics.
